channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Graph-Based Phonetic Error Correction of Noisy ASR
item.description: G-SPIN framework corrects ASR errors by combining phonetic graph modeling with language models to improve accuracy on semantically critical tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Small edits, large models: How Wikipedia advocacy shapes LLM values
item.description: Wikipedia animal welfare edits by activists substantially influence language model responses on related topics via heavy weighting in training data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AgentOdyssey: Open-Ended Long-Horizon Text Game Generation for Test-Time Continual Learning Agents
item.description: AgentOdyssey introduces a procedural text-game framework to evaluate agents' test-time continual learning abilities including exploration, knowledge acquisition, and long-horizon planning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Error-Aware TF-IDF Retrieval-Augmented Generation for ASR Error Correction
item.description: Error-aware TF-IDF retrieval-augmented generation reduces ASR word error rate from 23.06% to 18.83% with minimal inference latency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Perfect Detection, Failed Control: The Geometry of Knowing vs. Steering in Language Models
item.description: Perfect hallucination detection in language models doesn't enable control; detection and control directions geometrically misalign by approximately 83 degrees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Dustin: Draft-Augmented Sparse Verification for Efficient Long-Context Generation with Speculative Decoding
item.description: Dustin sparse verification framework optimizes long-context speculative decoding by identifying critical tokens, achieving 9.17x end-to-end speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LLM Performance on a Real, Double-Marked GCSE Benchmark
item.description: Large language models match examiner-level agreement on UK GCSE exams using a dataset of 32,534 real student responses spanning five subjects.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LLM-Based Scientific Peer Review: Methods, Benchmarks, and Reliability Challenges
item.description: LLMs can generate scientific review critiques but face reliability and security risks including prompt injection, data poisoning, and retrieval vulnerabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Dream at SemEval-2026 Task 13: SALSA for Single-Pass Machine-Generated Code Detection
item.description: A single-pass LLM classifier using SALSA for detecting machine-generated code achieves F1=0.789, substantially outperforming CodeBERT on out-of-distribution generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The cognitive, affective, and behavioral expression of self-stigma among people who use drugs in online substance use communities
item.description: LLM classification identifies self-stigma indicators in 5.3% of 72,115 Reddit posts from substance users across cognitive, affective, and behavioral domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hitting a Moving Target: Test-Time Adaptation for AI Text Detection under Continual Distribution Shift
item.description: Test-time adaptation leveraging semi-supervised learning makes AI text detectors robust to distribution shifts from new LLMs, adversarial humanization, and temporal drift.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What Intermediate Layers Know: Detecting Jailbreaks from Entropy Dynamics
item.description: Research shows entropy dynamics in intermediate LLM layers can detect jailbreak attacks, with evolving patterns more informative than static aggregate statistics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Towards Structuring an Arabic-English Machine-Readable Dictionary Using Parsing Expression Grammars
item.description: Using parsing expression grammars, researchers successfully structured the Arabic-English Al-Mawrid dictionary into machine-readable hierarchical components with plausible accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Automatic Generation of Highlights for Academic Paper Via Prompt-based Learning
item.description: Prompt-based learning enables language models like ChatGPT to generate academic paper highlights comparable to supervised methods without requiring labeled training data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Improved Large Language Diffusion Models
item.description: iLLaDA, an 8B bidirectional masked diffusion language model, improves substantially over its predecessor across general, mathematical, and code benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hybrid-IR: Dual-Path Hybrid Retrieval with Iterative Reasoning for Complex Medical Question Answering
item.description: Hybrid-IR combines graph-based and dense retrieval with iterative reasoning for improved complex medical question answering.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Efficient and Trainable Language Model Test-Time Scaling via Local Branch Routing
item.description: Local Branch Routing enables token-level test-time scaling for language models through lightweight routing over candidate futures, improving mathematical reasoning performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Memory Makes the Difference: Evaluating How Different Memory Roles Shape Conversational Agents
item.description: Research evaluates how different memory types in RAG-based conversational agents affect response quality, finding clarifying memories improve accuracy while irrelevant ones degrade relevance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Neural Machine Translation for Low-Resource Tangkhul--English
item.description: Researchers develop neural machine translation systems for low-resource Tangkhul-English, with ByT5-large achieving BLEU score of 39.97 on 38,336 parallel sentences.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Three Buddhist Vocabularies: Computational Stylometry of the English Pali Canon across Sutta, Vinaya, and Abhidhamma
item.description: Computational analysis of English Buddhist texts reveals Zipf-consistent vocabulary distributions and varying lexical diversity across Sutta, Vinaya, and Abhidhamma.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Story Operators: Decomposing the Original $\to$ Sequel Transformation in Embedding Space
item.description: Researchers analyze book-to-sequel transformations using sentence embeddings and PCA, revealing interpretable geometric operations that characterize different types of literary sequels.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Survey of Toxicity Detection and Mitigation Strategies for Multilingual Language Models
item.description: Survey synthesizes toxicity detection and detoxification approaches for multilingual LLMs, cataloging threat models, detection methods, and mitigation strategies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Introducing corpora Hlava Cor and Hlava AD: Human Label Variation in Coreference and Discourse Relations
item.description: Two Czech corpora with multiple annotations reveal human disagreement patterns in coreference and discourse relations, achieving approximately 60-65% inter-annotator agreement.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Next-Observation Prediction: Agent-Authored World Modeling for Sequential Decision Making
item.description: Agent-Authored World Modeling aligns agent training with decision needs rather than next-observation prediction, improving policy learning across environments.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PolicyAlign: Direct Policy-Based Safety Alignment for Large Language Models
item.description: PolicyAlign aligns LLMs to natural-language safety policies via policy-violating instruction synthesis and self-distillation without requiring pre-existing supervision data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reclaim Evaluation: A Lossy Memory Is Worse Than an Empty One
item.description: Language models with faulty memory perform worse than having no memory; source-first policies improve correctability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Probing in the Wild: A Case Study of Self-Supervised Speech Representations on Mandarin Sub-dialects with Unsupervised Articulatory Analysis
item.description: Researchers probe articulatory features in self-supervised speech models, finding uneven dialect variation across Mandarin sub-dialects driven by Beijing speech.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Optimizing Abstractive Summarization With Fine-Tuned PEGASUS
item.description: Fine-tuned PEGASUS achieves state-of-the-art abstractive summarization on XL-Sum English corpus with 4-15% ROUGE metric improvements over baseline.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Red Teaming Framework for Large Language Models: A Case Study on Faithfulness Evaluation
item.description: Red teaming framework using multi-role models exposes LLM faithfulness vulnerabilities, showing design choices outweigh parameter scaling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Reliable Is Your Jailbreak Judge? Calibration and Adversarial Robustness of Automated ASR Scoring
item.description: Automated judges for scoring LLM jailbreak attempts are unreliable, with safety classifiers and LLM judges exhibiting different failure modes and adversarial vulnerabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Dense Supervision Is Not Enough: The Readout Blind Spot in Looped Language Models
item.description: Dense supervision in looped models fails to control hidden-state scale with scale-invariant readouts; fixing requires explicit scale visibility or removal from recurrence.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Meta Idea to Advanced Mathematical Discovery -- Human-AI Co-Discovery of Sign-Embedding Quantum Algorithms
item.description: Human-AI collaboration developed sign-embedding quantum algorithms for matrix equations, with AI assisting in problem formulation and proof discovery.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] On-Device Neural Architecture Search
item.description: On-device neural architecture search adapts neural networks for real-time sensor data analysis on embedded systems with improved efficiency and accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLM Evolution as an Industry-Scale Ecosystem: A Lifecycle Perspective on Continual Learning
item.description: arXiv survey reformulates industrial LLM continual learning as versioned ecosystem problem, proposing five lifecycle principles for sustainable model evolution and capability transfer.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Spectral Phase Diagram for Binary Few-Shot Classification: Intrinsic Dimensionality, Geometric Saturation, and Representational Diagnosis
item.description: Researchers introduce a saturation index to determine when to stop collecting labeled data in few-shot classification, validated across 246 observations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Do Conservation Laws Survive Learned Representations? Certified Horizons for Latent World Models
item.description: Researchers develop methods to certify how long conservation laws hold in learned representations of physical world models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Conformal Orbit-Valid Trust Horizons for Equivariant World Models
item.description: Conformal methods certify trust horizons for equivariant latent world models, with equivariance properties ensuring reliability preservation across group transformations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Supervised Reinforcement Learning for the Coordination of Distributed Energy Resources
item.description: Supervised Reinforcement Learning for distributed energy resources combines supervised pre-training with offline-online fine-tuning, outperforming benchmarks with improved cost efficiency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Holographic Memory for Zero-Shot Compositional Reasoning in Knowledge Graphs: A Mechanistic Study of Where and Why It Fails
item.description: Holographic reduced representations perform well on single-hop knowledge graph queries but fail zero-shot compositional reasoning due to poor intermediate fact accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MacroLens: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Contextual Financial Reasoning under Macroeconomic Scenarios
item.description: MacroLens benchmark combines price, accounting, macroeconomic, and textual data across 4,416 equities for seven contextual forecasting and valuation tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] How Complexity Contributes to Learning Opacity in Machine Learning
item.description: Paper argues some neural network learning opacity may be irreducible due to dynamical complexity: weight initialization sensitivity, gradient feedback, training data sensitivity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Digital Twin-Driven Adaptive Sim-to-Real Alignment via Reinforcement Learning for Vibration-Based Bearing Health Monitoring Under Data Scarcity
item.description: Fault-specific reinforcement learning method aligns simulated bearing vibration signals to real data for improved health monitoring with limited fault examples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Continuous Power Forecasting: Practical Continual Learning for Real-World Energy Systems in Nonstationary Time Series
item.description: Continual learning enables power forecasting models to adapt to nonstationary conditions without requiring large historical datasets for retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Convex--Concave Quadratic Spectral Filtering for Graph Neural Networks
item.description: Spectral GNN method using compact quadratic filters improves graph learning selectivity and robustness compared to high-order polynomial alternatives.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Swarm-Inspired Generation of Collective Behaviors in Graph Dynamical Systems
item.description: Graph-dynamical framework learns generalizable local interaction rules for controllable collective behavior, demonstrated in synchronization control and multi-legged robot locomotion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reliable Conformal Prediction for Ordinal Classification Using the Ranked Probability Score
item.description: New conformal prediction method for ordinal classification uses ranked probability score to produce contiguous prediction sets with improved coverage-width balance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Enhancing Clinician Decision-Making via Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Expert Fusion for Stroke Rehabilitation
item.description: AI system achieves 94.2% accuracy in stroke rehabilitation assessment using uncertainty quantification and multi-model fusion to augment clinician judgment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Scalable Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning with Large Decision Models
item.description: A single transformer policy trained offline on trajectories from thousands of RL environments matches task-specific policies across robotics, autonomous driving, trading, and more.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Evidence for feature-specific error correction in LLMs
item.description: Researchers find evidence LLMs perform error correction in superposition, privileging certain feature directions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learning Dynamical Systems from Multiple Sparse Datasets: A Hierarchical Bayesian Modeling Approach
item.description: Hierarchical Bayesian framework enables efficient estimation of dynamical system parameters from multiple sparse datasets by modeling shared population structure.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] What Do Language Priors Contribute to Darcy-Flow Inversion? A Mechanistic Audit
item.description: Text embeddings reduce Darcy-flow inverse problem error by 81% through class-level constraints, enabling geological knowledge injection into learned solvers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Training Dynamics of Neural Software Defect Predictors under Coupled Data-Quality Issues
item.description: Study investigates how coupled class imbalance and overlap affect neural network training dynamics in software defect prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Frequency Domain Reservoir Computing
item.description: FRESCO reduces Echo State Network complexity from O(N²) to O(N) by performing recurrent computations in the frequency domain.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Don't Go Breaking My LLM: The Impact of Pruning Attention Layers on Explanation Faithfulness and Confidence Calibration
item.description: Pruning attention layers in LLMs maintains accuracy but degrades explanation faithfulness and confidence calibration.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quantifying Explainable AI-introduced signal noise on ECG data with Spectral Entropy
item.description: Spectral entropy is proposed to quantify noise introduced by explainability techniques when analyzing deep learning models for ECG arrhythmia detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Why Do Accumulated Transformations Extrapolate?
item.description: Accumulated orthogonal transformations improve length extrapolation via finite mixing windows, but must eventually degrade as context grows.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Auto-Configured Explainable Graph Neural Networks for Multi-Site Pollution Prediction
item.description: GNN-based method with dynamic graph construction and hybrid loss improves particulate matter prediction across multiple air pollution monitoring sites using explainable AI techniques.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CKM-Driven Communication-Aware UAV Intelligent Trajectory Optimization for Urban Inspection
item.description: UAV path planning framework uses channel knowledge maps and reinforcement learning to optimize inspection trajectories while maintaining communication reliability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Closed-Loop Graph Algorithm Execution with Small Language Models: Step Accuracy and Rollout Reliability
item.description: Small language models reliably execute simple graph algorithms like traversal and coloring but struggle with weighted algorithms due to error accumulation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Single Stepsize Suffices for Unprojected Linear TD(0): Simultaneous Robust and Fast Rates via Polyak--Ruppert Averaging
item.description: Linear TD(0) with single stepsize and Polyak-Ruppert averaging achieves robust and fast convergence rates without knowing curvature parameter.
